attempted_job.description: Nitronyl nitroxides are stable radicals in which the unpaired electron is coupled to two equivalent nitrogen nuclei. How many lines will appear in the EPR spectrum of a solution of a rigid nitronyl nitroxide diradical with J >> a?

A 20-month old clings nervously to her mother and does not want to explore a new play environment. She suddenly pushes her mother away and does not engage in any hugs her mother tries to give her. The infant’s facial expression shows anxiety but she looks away from her mother. The mother turns away from the baby as well. This child most likely has which type of attachment to her mother?

This question refers to the following information.
Read the documents below.
Source 1
If then by the Use of Machines, the Manufacture of Cotton, an Article which we import, and are supplied with from other Countries, and which can everywhere be procured on equal Terms, has met with such amazing Success, may not greater Advantages be reasonably expected from cultivating to the utmost the Manufacture of Wool, the Produce of our own Island, an Article in Demand in all Countries, almost the universal Clothing of Mankind?
In the Manufacture of Woollens, the Scribbling Mill, the Spinning Frame, and the Fly Shuttle, have reduced manual Labour nearly One third, and each of them at its-first Introduction carried an Alarm to the Work People, yet each has contributed to advance the Wages and to increase the Trade, so that if an Attempt was now made to deprive us of the Use of them, there is no Doubt, but every Person engaged in the Business, would exert himself to defend them.
—Statement by the Cloth Merchants of Leeds, 1791
Source 2
Come, cropper lads of high renown,
Who love to drink good ale that's brown,
And strike each haughty tyrant down,
With hatchet, pike, and gun!
Oh, the cropper lads for me,
The gallant lads for me,
Who with lusty stroke,
The shear frames broke,
The cropper lads for me!
What though the specials still advance,
And soldiers nightly round us prance;
The cropper lads still lead the dance,
With hatchet, pike, and gun!
Oh, the cropper lads for me,
The gallant lads for me,
Who with lusty stroke
The shear frames broke,
The cropper lads for me!
—Luddite Song, The Cropper's Song, c. 1812
Which of the following economic theories is Source 1 above referencing in support of the expansion and use of machines?
Why do economists make seasonal adjustmentsin economic data before trying to isolate long-term trends?
options: To differentiate between urban and rural economic activity
To gain a more accurate picture of the actual trend
To adjust for variations in weather patterns
To account for short-term market fluctuations
To prepare for upcoming financial crises
To correct for data collection errors
To align with fiscal policy changes
attempted_job.correct_answer: To gain a more accurate picture of the actual trend

attempted_job.description:
Which of the following statements are true concerning information criteria?

(i) Adjusted R-squared is an information criterion

(ii) If the residual sum of squares falls when an additional term is added, the value of the information criterion will fall

(iii) Akaike's information criterion always leads to model orders that are at least as large as those of Schwarz's information criterion

(iv) Akaike's information criterion is consistent
Which of the following is true about the pure public good?
options: The marginal cost of providing an additional unit of the good to additional citizens is zero.
The consumption of public goods leads to depletion of resources.
The public good can be consumed by only a limited number of consumers at a time.
One more unit of a public good for some consumers means one less unit for other consumers.
Consumers can be excluded from its benefits by not paying for it.
The benefits of public goods can be privatized by corporations.
The good cannot be divided into discrete units.
The marginal cost of providing an additional unit of the good to additional citizens is greater than zero.
Public goods can only be consumed by those who pay for them.
Public goods are always provided by government agencies.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The good cannot be divided into discrete units.

attempted_job.description: An important characteristic of services is that they are produced and consumed by people, simultaneously, as a single event. One of the outcomes of this unique process is that it is exceedingly difficult to standardize the delivery of services around the blueprint model. Which characteristic of service is this referred to?
The United States produces rice in a competitive market. With free trade the world price is lower than the domestic price. What must be true?
options: The United States begins to import rice to make up for a domestic shortage.
The United States begins to export rice to make up for a domestic shortage.
The United States begins to import rice to eliminate a domestic surplus.
The United States begins to export rice to eliminate a domestic surplus.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The United States begins to import rice to make up for a domestic shortage.

A man was on a date when he realized that he didn't have enough money to pay the bill. The man excused himself and went to the men's room. The man decided to set fire to the waste paper in a trash container. He hoped that the fire would serve as a diversion so he and his date could run out of the restaurant without paying. He set fire to the waste paper and then went back to his table. As he anxiously waited, the maitre d' grabbed the man and told him that he had seen what occurred in the bathroom. The maitre d', who had been seated in a bathroom stall, saw the man, through a crack in the door, set the fire. The maitre d' extinguished the fire, which had charred a portion of the ceiling. This jurisdiction defines arson as the malicious burning of any structure. If charged with arson, the man should be found
options: not guilty, because he did not intend to burn down the restaurant.
not guilty, because the fire only charred a portion of the ceiling.
not guilty, because he did not have the intent to cause harm to any individuals.
guilty, because the act caused damage to the restaurant's ceiling.
guilty, because he was reckless as to the restaurant's being damaged by fire.
guilty, because he set the fire for the purpose of committing an unlawful act.
guilty, because the fire was intentionally started, regardless of the purpose.
not guilty, because the fire did not result in significant damage to the structure.
not guilty, because he did not directly set fire to the structure itself.
guilty, because he set fire with an intent to create a diversion.
attempted_job.correct_answer: guilty, because he was reckless as to the restaurant's being damaged by fire.

Indicate whether the following economic events represent a change in supply or a change in quantity supplied. a) Cold weather in Florida destroys a great deal of the orange crop. Therefore there are fewer oranges in the country's supermarkets. b) The cost of limes increase. As a result, bar owners raise the price of gin and tonics. c) The price of leather shoes goes up. This induces manufacturers of leather shoes to increase production.
options: a) None of the above, b) Change in supply, c) Change in quantity supplied
a) Change in quantity supplied, b) Change in quantity supplied, c) Change in supply
a) None of the above, b) None of the above, c) None of the above
a) Change in supply, b) Change in quantity supplied, c) None of the above
a) Change in quantity supplied, b) None of the above, c) Change in supply
a) Change in supply, b) Change in quantity supplied, c) Change in supply
a) Change in supply, b) Change in supply, c) Change in quantity supplied
a) Change in quantity supplied, b) Change in supply, c) Change in supply
a) Change in supply, b) Change in quantity supplied, c) Change in quantity supplied
a) Change in quantity supplied, b) Change in supply, c) Change in quantity supplied
attempted_job.correct_answer: a) Change in supply, b) Change in supply, c) Change in quantity supplied

attempted_job.description: What distinguishes the trade patterns of the Mesolithic from those of the Upper Paleolithic?
options: in the Mesolithic, goods traveled less far and fewer goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled farther but the same number of goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled less far but the same number of goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled the same distance but more goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled farther but fewer goods were exchanged
in the Upper Paleolithic, goods traveled less far but more goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled the same distance but fewer goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled less far but more goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled farther and more goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled the same distance and the same number of goods were exchanged
attempted_job.correct_answer: in the Mesolithic, goods traveled less far but more goods were exchanged

Sociopolitical, Overuse of resources, Overpopulation, Over pollution
Economic, Overuse of resources, Overpopulation, Over pollution,
Economic, Overuse of resources, Stable population, Over pollution
Environmental, Balanced use of resources, Overpopulation, Unsustainability
Economic, Underuse of resources, Underpopulation, Unsustainability
attempted_job.correct_answer: Environmental, Underuse of resources, Overpopulation, Over pollution

found by adding product demand curves both horizontally and vertically

ii) The pacf for an MA(q) model will in general be non-zero beyond lag q

During the latter part of their first year, children’s language- learning ability changes in thar they
options: begin to utter words in an unsystematic and random manner
begin producing more language than they can comprehend
begin to utter: their first words in a systematic progression determined by selective reinforcement of babbling
lose the ability to understand words in their own language
begin to be sensitive to rhythms in speech patterns
lose the ability to perceive any sound distinctions
are able to perceive sound distinctions in multiple languages
become less able to perceive sound distinctions not made in their own language
attempted_job.correct_answer: become less able to perceive sound distinctions not made in their own language

attempted_job.description: Which of the following statements is correct concerning the "reasonable person" standard in tort law?
options: The reasonable person standard applies only to people with a high level of education.
The reasonable person standard applies only to people without disabilities.
The reasonable person standard varies from person to person.
The reasonable person standard requires that a person act as would the average person in the community.
A person with a mental disability must act as would a person with the same mental disability.
A person with a physical disability must act as would a reasonable person with the same disability.
The reasonable person standard focuses on the defendant's subjective mental state rather than on the defendant's behavior
The reasonable person standard is based solely on the defendant's age.
A person with a mental disability is not subject to the reasonable person standard.
attempted_job.correct_answer: A person with a physical disability must act as would a reasonable person with the same disability.

A state built a casino and issued bonds to finance its construction. On five occasions, there were episodes of violence in various casinos in the state. The state police attributed the violence to greed and fear at the casinos. To prevent such violence, the state legislature passes a statute prohibiting all gambling at privately owned casinos in the state. Is this law likely to be held constitutional if most casinos in the state were owned by those from out-of-state?
options: Yes, because the act was expressly authorized by the state legislature.
Yes, but only if the local interest in safety outweighs the burden of interstate commerce.
No, because out-of-state casinos are part of interstate commerce.
No, because the statute violates the due process rights of the owners of the casinos.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Yes, but only if the local interest in safety outweighs the burden of interstate commerce.

attempted_job.description: Mr. Howard owns a house worth $16,500 which is assessed at the rate of 22%. His tax rates are as follows: $.41 per $100 for a local college fund $.02 per $100 for special education $.001 per $100 for health and welfare $.089 per $100 for community construction How much tax does Mr. Howard pay for each of these needs? What was the total tax?

Carruthers approaches questions about the moral standing of nonhuman animals from the perspective of which of the following moral theories?

In a study of brain development, two groups of rats were reared in a laboratory setting. In Group I, each rat was raised in isolation with no toys, while in Group II, rats were divided into small groups and given toys to play with. Which of the following statements most accurately reflects the probable findings of this study?
options: The rats raised in Group II will have forebrains that weigh more than those of the rats raised in Group I.
The brains of the rats raised in Group I will show increased signs of stress compared to the brains of the rats raised in Group II.
The brains of the rats raised in Group I will not be noticeably different from the brains of the rats raised in Group II.
The rats raised in Group I will have forebrains that weigh less than those of the rats raised in Group II.
The brains of the rats raised in Group I will consist of more white matter, while the brains of the rats raised in Group II will consist of more gray matter.
The rats raised in Group I will have forebrains that weigh more than those of the rats raised in Group II.
The rats raised in Group II will have smaller brains overall due to increased play activity.
The rats raised in Group I will have less developed neural pathways than those of the rats raised in Group II.
The brains of the rats raised in Group II will consist of more white matter, while the brains of the rats raised in Group I will consist of more gray matter.
The rats raised in Group I will have forebrains that are the same weight as those of the rats raised in Group II.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The rats raised in Group I will have forebrains that weigh less than those of the rats raised in Group II.

attempted_job.description: New City has an annual budget of $4,221,890.49. Its property has a total assessed valuation of $150,781,803.21. What is the city's tax rate if other estimated receipts total $385,000.
What are the symptoms of a paranoiddisorder ?Discuss the threetypes of this disorder.
options: The symptoms of paranoid disorder are persistent, persecutory delusions or delusions of jealousy. The three types of paranoid disorders are paranoia, shared paranoia, and paranoid state.
Symptoms include hallucinations and mood swings. The three types are delusional disorder, shared delusion, and paranoia
Symptoms are delusions and hallucinations. Types include paranoid schizophrenia, paranoid personality disorder, and delusional disorder.
Symptoms are distrust and communication difficulties. The types include paranoid anxiety, paranoid personality disorder, and paranoid psychosis.
The symptoms include obsessive thoughts and compulsive behaviors. The types are shared paranoia, paranoid personality disorder, and paranoia
Symptoms are anxiety and depression. Types include shared paranoia, paranoia, and paranoid personality disorder
The symptoms include chronic anxiety and mistrust. The types are paranoid schizophrenia, paranoia, and paranoid psychosis.
The symptoms include extreme suspicion and social isolation. The types are delusional disorder, paranoia, and paranoid anxiety disorder.
Symptoms include persistent fear and social withdrawal. The types are paranoid psychosis, shared paranoid disorder, and paranoid depression.
The symptoms of paranoid disorder are delusions of grandeur and auditory hallucinations. The three types are paranoid personality disorder, paranoid schizophrenia, and paranoia.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The symptoms of paranoid disorder are persistent, persecutory delusions or delusions of jealousy. The three types of paranoid disorders are paranoia, shared paranoia, and paranoid state.

Let P be a procedure that for some inputs calls itself (i.e., is recursive). If P is guaranteed to terminate, which of the following statements must be true?
I. P has a local variable.
II. P has an execution path where it does not call itself.
III. P either refers to a global variable or has at least one parameter.
options: P has an execution path where it does not call itself and refers to a global variable
P has a local variable and refers to a global variable

attempted_job.description: Studies into the etiology of Schizophrenia indicated a genetic predisposition to the condition but other factors are involved. From your knowledge of the subject identify which of the following statements is incorrect.
options: Excess dopamine or sensitivity to dopamine could be a contributory factor.
A lower rate of Schizophrenia diagnosed among Africa Americans.
Schizophrenia is primarily caused by poor lifestyle choices
Schizophrenia can be completely cured through psychotherapy
All schizophrenia patients exhibit severe personality splitting
attempted_job.correct_answer: A lower rate of Schizophrenia diagnosed among Africa Americans.

This question refers to the following information.
"Thereupon it was declared by the above-mentioned representatives of the aforesaid King and Queen of Castile, Leon, Aragon, Sicily, Granada, etc., and of the aforesaid King of Portugal and the Algarves, etc.:
[I.] That, whereas a certain controversy exists between the said lords, their constituents, as to what lands, of all those discovered in the ocean sea up to the present day, the date of this treaty, pertain to each one of the said parts respectively; therefore, for the sake of peace and concord, and for the preservation of the relationship and love of the said King of Portugal for the said King and Queen of Castile, Aragon, etc., it being the pleasure of their Highnesses, they, their said representatives, acting in their name and by virtue of their powers herein described, covenanted and agreed that a boundary or straight line be determined and drawn north and south, from pole to pole, on the said ocean sea, from the Arctic to the Antarctic pole. This boundary or line shall be drawn straight, as aforesaid, at a distance of three hundred and seventy leagues west of the Cape Verde Islands, being calculated by degrees, or by any other manner as may be considered the best and readiest, provided the distance shall be no greater than abovesaid. And all lands, both islands and mainlands, found and discovered already, or to be found and discovered hereafter, by the said King of Portugal and by his vessels on this side of the said line and bound determined as above, toward the east, in either north or south latitude, on the eastern side of the said bound provided the said bound is not crossed, shall belong to, and remain in the possession of, and pertain forever to, the said King of Portugal and his successors. And all other lands, both islands and mainlands, found or to be found hereafter, discovered or to be discovered hereafter, which have been discovered or shall be discovered by the said King and Queen of Castile, Aragon, etc., and by their vessels, on the western side of the said bound, determined as above, after having passed the said bound toward the west, in either its north or south latitude, shall belong to, and remain in the possession of, and pertain forever to, the said King and Queen of Castile, Leon, etc., and to their successors."
Treaty of Tordesillas, 1494
Which of the following inferences best explains the reason the King and Queen of Castile and Aragon insisted on Spanish control of territory west of the demarcation line?
options: The Spanish desired to convert the Barbary states to Catholicism.
The Spanish believed Portugal did not know about their discovery of the New World.
The Spanish wanted to preserve the independence of Native states in the New World as a buffer against Portuguese expansion.
The Spanish thought India would be harder to conquer than the New World.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The Spanish believed Portugal did not know about their discovery of the New World.

attempted_job.description: Give a brief history of the study of social behavior.
options: Four main periods: the classical, medieval, industrial, and information periods.
Five distinct periods: the philosophical, statistical, analytical, modern, and post-modern periods.
Only one period: the analytical period.
Six distinct periods: the prehistoric, ancient, medieval, renaissance, industrial, and contemporary periods.
Seven periods: the mythological, philosophical, theological, metaphysical, empirical, analytical, and computational periods.
Four distinct periods: the philosophical, statistical, analytical, and modern periods.
Two main periods: the pre-Enlightenment and Enlightenment periods.
Two main periods: the philosophical and statistical periods.
Three distinct periods: the philosophical, statistical and analytical periods.
Three distinct periods: the pre-scientific, scientific, and digital periods.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Three distinct periods: the philosophical, statistical and analytical periods.

attempted_job.description: Can intelligence tests be used for diagnosing psychopathology ?
options: Intelligence test scores can be used for diagnosing psychopathology.
Intelligence test scores are primarily used to assess cognitive ability and do not relate to psychopathological conditions.
Intelligence test scores provide definitive evidence of the absence of psychopathology.
Intelligence tests can diagnose psychopathology when combined with genetic testing.
Psychopathology can be inferred from intelligence test scores with the help of behavioral analysis.
Intelligence test scores can determine the type of psychopathology.
It has not proven possible to make psychiatric diagnoses on the basis of intelligence test scores.
Intelligence test scores are only used for diagnosing learning disabilities, not other forms of psychopathology.
Intelligence test scores can accurately predict future psychopathological behavior.
attempted_job.correct_answer: It has not proven possible to make psychiatric diagnoses on the basis of intelligence test scores.

attempted_job.description: Within a cell, the amount of protein synthesized using a given mRNA molecule depends in part on which of the following?
options: The amount of protein synthesized is determined by the rate of cell division.
The age of the cell will determine how much protein is synthesized.
The amount of protein synthesized is determined by the amount of DNA replication.
The location and number of ribosomes in a cell will solely determine how much protein is synthesized.
The type of cell will solely determine how much protein is synthesized.
DNA methylation suppresses the expression of genes.
The size of the nucleus will determine how much protein is synthesized.
The number of chromosomes in a cell determines how much protein is synthesized.
Transcription factors mediate the binding of RNA polymerase and the initiation of transcription, which will determine how much protein is manufactured.
The speed with which mRNA is degraded will determine how much protein is synthesized.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The speed with which mRNA is degraded will determine how much protein is synthesized.

attempted_job.description: List and compare the tissues that support and hold together theother tissues of the body.
options: Connective tissue is a single group of tissues that encapsulates organs without providing structural support.
The only function of connective tissue is to transport nutrients throughout the body.
Connective tissue is divided into two groups: bone and cartilage.
Connective tissue includes three types: muscle, nerve, and epithelial tissues.
Connective tissue is mainly composed of fluids and lacks solid structures like bone or cartilage.

Connective tissue functions to support and hold together structures of the body. They are classified into four groups by structure and/or function: bone, cartilage, blood, and fibrous connective tissue.
Connective tissue is categorized into five groups: bone, cartilage, blood, fibrous connective tissue, and adipose tissue.
Fibrous connective tissue doesn't contribute to the support and holding of other tissues.
Connective tissue only supports other tissues, it doesn't hold them together.
Connective tissues are not classified; all connective tissues have the same function and structure.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Connective tissue functions to support and hold together structures of the body. They are classified into four groups by structure and/or function: bone, cartilage, blood, and fibrous connective tissue.

attempted_job.description: Why do economists make seasonal adjustmentsin economic data before trying to isolate long-term trends?
options: To differentiate between urban and rural economic activity
To gain a more accurate picture of the actual trend
To adjust for variations in weather patterns
To account for short-term market fluctuations
To prepare for upcoming financial crises
To correct for data collection errors
To align with fiscal policy changes
attempted_job.correct_answer: To gain a more accurate picture of the actual trend

attempted_job.description: Is the cumulative distribution function of the standard gaussian distribution $F(x)=1/\sqrt{2 \pi} \int_{-\infty}^x e^{-t^2/2} dt$ is log-concave?  Return 1 for yes and 0 for no.

A 67-year-old man with Parkinson disease is admitted to the hospital for treatment of pneumonia. The patient's daughter, who is visiting the patient, says he has had increased lethargy for the past day and decreased ambulation during the past 6 months. She also says that there are times during the day when his tremors increase in severity, although he continues to care for himself at home. Medical history is also remarkable for hypertension. Medications include hydrochlorothiazide, atenolol, levodopa, and carbidopa. He is 168 cm (5 ft 6 in) tall and weighs 78 kg (172 lb); BMI is 28 kg/m2 . Vital signs are temperature 38.9°C (102.0°F), pulse 60/min supine and 68/min standing, respirations 22/min, and blood pressure 100/60 mm Hg supine and 80/50 mm Hg standing. The patient appears ill and older than his stated age. He is fully oriented but lethargic. Auscultation of the chest discloses rhonchi in the right mid lung field. Abdominal examination discloses no abnormalities. Neurologic examination discloses masked facies, bradykinesia, and cogwheel rigidity; gait was not assessed on admission. Chest x-ray shows a right lower lobe infiltrate. ECG shows no abnormalities. Appropriate intravenous antibiotic therapy is initiated. Prior to discharge, which of the following is the most appropriate step?
options: Place a percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) tube
Obtain CT scan of the chest

attempted_job.description: If OLS is applied separately to each equation that is part of a simultaneous system, the resulting estimates will be
options: Biased but inconsistent for one equation only
It is impossible to apply OLS to equations that are part of a simultaneous system
It is possible to apply OLS to equations that are part of a simultaneous system, but the results will be unpredictable

attempted_job.description: A 25-year-old man is brought to the emergency department because of a 6-day history of fever, severe muscle pain, and diffuse, painful swelling of his neck, underarms, and groin area. The symptoms began after returning from a camping trip in New Mexico. He appears ill and lethargic and can barely answer questions. His temperature is 39.2°C (102.5°F), pulse is 120/min, respirations are 22/min, and blood pressure is 110/70 mm Hg. Physical examination shows generalized scattered black maculae. Examination of the right upper extremity shows an erythematous, solid, tender mass on the underside of the upper extremity just above the elbow; the mass is draining blood and necrotic material. The most effective antibiotic for this patient’s disorder will interfere with which of the following cellular processes or enzymes?
The body mass index (BMI) of vegetarians differs on average from meat-eaters by how much?

attempted_job.description: Which of the following is true about the pure public good?
options: The marginal cost of providing an additional unit of the good to additional citizens is zero.
The consumption of public goods leads to depletion of resources.
The public good can be consumed by only a limited number of consumers at a time.
One more unit of a public good for some consumers means one less unit for other consumers.
Consumers can be excluded from its benefits by not paying for it.
The benefits of public goods can be privatized by corporations.
The good cannot be divided into discrete units.
The marginal cost of providing an additional unit of the good to additional citizens is greater than zero.
Public goods can only be consumed by those who pay for them.
Public goods are always provided by government agencies.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The good cannot be divided into discrete units.

attempted_job.description: What are the symptoms of a paranoiddisorder ?Discuss the threetypes of this disorder.
options: The symptoms of paranoid disorder are persistent, persecutory delusions or delusions of jealousy. The three types of paranoid disorders are paranoia, shared paranoia, and paranoid state.
Symptoms include hallucinations and mood swings. The three types are delusional disorder, shared delusion, and paranoia
Symptoms are delusions and hallucinations. Types include paranoid schizophrenia, paranoid personality disorder, and delusional disorder.
Symptoms are distrust and communication difficulties. The types include paranoid anxiety, paranoid personality disorder, and paranoid psychosis.
The symptoms include obsessive thoughts and compulsive behaviors. The types are shared paranoia, paranoid personality disorder, and paranoia
Symptoms are anxiety and depression. Types include shared paranoia, paranoia, and paranoid personality disorder
The symptoms include chronic anxiety and mistrust. The types are paranoid schizophrenia, paranoia, and paranoid psychosis.
The symptoms include extreme suspicion and social isolation. The types are delusional disorder, paranoia, and paranoid anxiety disorder.
Symptoms include persistent fear and social withdrawal. The types are paranoid psychosis, shared paranoid disorder, and paranoid depression.
The symptoms of paranoid disorder are delusions of grandeur and auditory hallucinations. The three types are paranoid personality disorder, paranoid schizophrenia, and paranoia.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The symptoms of paranoid disorder are persistent, persecutory delusions or delusions of jealousy. The three types of paranoid disorders are paranoia, shared paranoia, and paranoid state.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following is recommended for cleaning the mouth?
Who discovered the first virus?
An ideal solution is a
options: mixture where the solvents cannot be dissolved in any ratio
mixture that reacts violently when combined
mixture where the solvents repel each other
mixture where the potential energy of the mixture is the same as that of the individual solvents
mixture that changes color when combined
mixture that decreases the potential energy when combined
mixture that increases the potential energy when combined
mixture that has the same physical properties as the individual solvents
attempted_job.correct_answer: mixture where the potential energy of the mixture is the same as that of the individual solvents

attempted_job.description: A landlord is the owner in fee simple of a tract of land on which is situated a large office building. The landlord leases the land and building thereon to a tenant for a term of seven years, commencing on August 1, 2001, and terminating at midnight on July 31, 2008. The lease contains the following provisions:"(1) The tenant covenants to pay the rent of $750 per month on the first day of each month. "After three years of the lease had expired, the tenant assigned the entire balance of the lease period to an attorney, who took immediate possession of the leased property. Then in 2005, the attorney assigned his leasehold interest to a doctor. The doctor went into possession, but failed to pay any rent for two months. After the doctor failed to make his rental payments for the first two months of his lease, the landlord brought suit against the tenant to recover for the unpaid rent. Judgment should be for
options: the tenant, because he was not directly responsible for the doctor's failure to pay rent.
the landlord, because the tenant and the assignees breached the lease agreement.
the tenant, because the landlord did not take action when the doctor failed to pay rent.
the tenant, because the doctor, as assignee, would only be held liable.
the tenant, because the landlord did not formally approve the assignments.
the landlord, because he did not object to the assignments.
the landlord, because the doctor, as the final assignee, failed to fulfill his obligation to pay rent.
the tenant, because his assignment to the attorney constituted a novation, thereby extinguishing his obligation to pay rent.
the landlord, because the tenant's contractual obligation under the lease survived the assignments.
the landlord, because the attorney failed to pay rent for two months.
attempted_job.correct_answer: the landlord, because the tenant's contractual obligation under the lease survived the assignments.

attempted_job.description: A teenage male was on trial on charges of illegally entering an owner's property and stealing a car. The witness can only confirm for the prosecution that a car was stolen, the model and type of car, and that it was missing from the property on a certain date. The defense asks witness "isn't it possible that my client did not steal the car, that someone else did it, and that the police are lying about the charges against him? The prosecution objected to the question. Will the court likely sustain the objection?
options: No, as it is essential for the defense to create reasonable doubt in the minds of the jury.
Yes, because the question is not directly related to the charges against the defendant.
Yes, because this is an objectionable question based on pure speculation.
No, because the defense is merely suggesting a possibility, not stating it as fact.
No, because the defense has the right to question every aspect of the prosecution's case.
Yes, because it is not permissible to question the credibility of the police without substantial supporting evidence.
No, questioning about the possibility of other perpetrators, or about police misconduct, is always permissible.
No, because the witness may know something that he is not revealing and for that reason, a cross-examiner is given wide latitude in questioning.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Yes, because this is an objectionable question based on pure speculation.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following is a function of biotin?
options: Oxidation of glucose to carbon dioxide and water
Synthesis of fatty acids from acetyl-CoA
Decarboxylation of amino acids to form amine neurotransmitters

attempted_job.description:
Use the following key to translate the given formula of PL to natural, English sentences.
A: Marina reads a Percy Jackson book.
B: Izzy plays Minecraft.
C: Emily stops working.
D: Russell makes dinner.
E: Ashleigh stops by.
(A ∨ ~E) ≡ (D • ~B)
options: Either Marina reading a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh not stopping by is a necessary condition for both Russell not making dinner and Izzy not playing Minecraft.
Either Marina reads a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh does not stop by just in case both Russell makes dinner and Izzy doesn't play Minecraft.
Marina reads a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh doesn't stop by only if Russell doesn't make dinner and Izzy plays Minecraft.
Marina reads a Percy Jackson book unless Ashleigh stops by if, and only if, both Russell makes dinner and Izzy doesn't play Minecraft.
Marina reads a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh does not stop by only when Russell is making dinner and Izzy is not playing Minecraft.
Either Marina reads a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh doesn't stop by whenever Russell makes dinner and Izzy doesn't play Minecraft.
Marina reading a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh not stopping by is a necessary condition for Russell not making dinner and Izzy playing Minecraft.
Either Marina reading a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh not stopping by is a necessary and sufficient condition for Russell making dinner and Izzy playing Minecraft.
Either Marina reading a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh not stopping by is a necessary condition for both Russell making dinner and Izzy not playing Minecraft.
Either Marina reading a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh not stopping by is a necessary and sufficient condition for Russell not making dinner and Izzy playing Minecraft.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Either Marina reads a Percy Jackson book or Ashleigh does not stop by just in case both Russell makes dinner and Izzy doesn't play Minecraft.

attempted_job.description: A father died leaving a will by which he devised a 100-acre tract to his daughter. At the time of the father's death, he believed that he owned all of the 100-acre tract. However, the father actually owned 95 acres of the tract. The other five acres were owned by a farmer. After taking possession of the tract, the daughter executed and delivered a warranty deed to a buyer, purporting to convey all 100 acres of the tract. The agreed purchase price was $100,000. According to the terms of the sale, the buyer paid the daughter a down payment of $25,000, with the unpaid portion of the purchase price (i. e. , $75,000) secured by a mortgage. The mortgage instrument described the property interest as covering all 100 acres of the tract. After the daughter buyer transaction was completed, the farmer came forward and informed the parties that she was the true record owner of five acres of the 100-acre tract. Upon the farmer's threat to sue to quiet title, the daughter negotiated a deal whereby she paid the farmer $5,000 for the five-acre tract. As part of their agreement, the farmer executed and delivered a quitclaim deed quitclaiming to the buyer all of her interest in the five-acre parcel. Thereafter, the buyer defaulted on the mortgage debt, and the daughter properly consummated foreclosure proceedings, becoming the purchaser of the tract at her own foreclosure sale. The description of the deed in the foreclosure sale referred to the tract as consisting of all 100 acres. After the foreclosure sale was finalized, the buyer brought suit claiming title in fee simple to the five-acre tract, formerly owned by the farmer. In an appropriate action to determine the title to the said five-acre tract, the buyer will
options: lose, because the buyer did not have a legal claim to the five-acre tract
lose, because the foreclosure proceedings were properly executed
win, because the farmer's threat to sue to quiet title was not legally binding
win, because the deed from the farmer to the buyer was a quitclaim deed.
win, because the quitclaim deed from the farmer to the buyer was subsequent to the deed from the daughter to the buyer and to the mortgage.
lose, because the buyer defaulted on the mortgage debt
win, because the daughter purchased the tract at her own foreclosure sale
lose, because the doctrine of after-acquired title controls.
win, because the mortgage instrument described the property interest as covering all 100 acres of the tract.
attempted_job.correct_answer: lose, because the doctrine of after-acquired title controls.

attempted_job.description: Biologists around the world have increased efforts at conservation. Monitoring wildlife populations is important so that appropriate management efforts can be implemented, but such monitoring is often difficult. One study found a cheap and easy way to estimate the number of nesting sites of terns (a type of seabird) by monitoring the number of calls heard per minute. More calls happen when there are more birds, as one would expect. In fact, it turned out that the number of calls explained 71% of the variation in the abundance of nests between breeding sites. Which of the following statements is correct about the correlation between the number of calls and the abundance of nests?
A family had eight children. The ages were 9, 11, 8, 15, 14, 12, 17, 14. (a) Find the measures of central tendency for the data. (b) Find the range of the data.

A 26-foot ladder leans against a building so that its foot moves away from the building at the rate of 3 feet per second. When the foot of the ladder is 10 feet from the building, the top is moving down at the rate of r feet per second, where r is

John divided his souvenir hat pins into two piles. The two piles had an equal number of pins. He gave his brother one-half of one-third of one pile. John had 66 pins left. How many pins did John originally have?
The Law of Diminishing Marginal Returns is responsible for
options: MC that first falls, but eventually rises, as output increases.
AP that first falls, but eventually rises, as output increases.
TC that first falls, but eventually rises, as output increases.
AVC that first rises, but eventually falls, as output increases.
MP that first falls, but eventually rises, as output increases.
AFC that first rises, but eventually falls, as output increases.
TP that first rises, but eventually falls, as output increases.
AFC that first falls, but eventually rises, as output increases.
attempted_job.correct_answer: MC that first falls, but eventually rises, as output increases.

attempted_job.description: Statement 1 | Every group of order 159 is cyclic. Statement 2 | Every group of order 102 has a nontrivial proper normal subgroup.
options: Cannot be determined, Cannot be determined

attempted_job.description: According to Rawls, the two parts of his theory:
options: can be proven false with logical reasoning.
are independent: You can accept either part without accepting the other.
are based purely on empirical evidence.
are not open to debate or disagreement.
cannot be justified via rational means.
can only be justified through emotional appeal.
are based on personal preferences and biases.
are dependent on each other, but can be accepted separately.
are interdependent: You cannot accept one part without accepting the other.
attempted_job.correct_answer: are independent: You can accept either part without accepting the other.

attempted_job.description: Mill claims that the principle of utility:
options: can only be proven through extensive observation and study.
can be proven from principles that are known empirically.
can be proven, but only under certain conditions.
is inherently flawed and therefore cannot be proven.
is a theory that has been widely disproven.
can be proven, but this is unique to the principle of utility.
cannot be proven, but this is common to all first principles.
can be proven, but only by using a complex mathematical model.
cannot be proven, and this is a unique problem for the theory.
can be proven from self-evident principles.
attempted_job.correct_answer: cannot be proven, but this is common to all first principles.

attempted_job.description: Which is true about Batch Normalization?
options: Batch Normalization does not provide any benefits when used with ReLU activation function.
Batch Normalization should only be used for fully-connected layers in neural networks.
Batch Normalization eliminates the need for dropout in a neural network.
The standard weight initialization must be changed when using Batch Normalization.
Batch Normalization is equivalent to Layer Normalization for convolutional neural networks.
The bias parameter of affine layers becomes redundant if a batch normalization layer follows immediately afterward.
Batch Normalization makes the learning rate redundant.
Batch Normalization adjusts the weights of the network instead of the activations.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The bias parameter of affine layers becomes redundant if a batch normalization layer follows immediately afterward.

attempted_job.description: As the tides change, the water level in a bay varies sinusoidally. At high tide today at 8 A.M., the water level was 15 feet; at low tide, 6 hours later at 2 P.M., it was 3 feet. How fast, in feet per hour, was the water level dropping at noon today?
The information that family members continuously exchange and that helps minimize deviation and maintain the family's stability is referred to as ________ feedback.
For the $\mathrm{H}_2$ ground electronic state, $D_0=4.4781 \mathrm{eV}$. Find $\Delta H_0^{\circ}$ for $\mathrm{H}_2(g) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}(g)$ in $\mathrm{kJ} / \mathrm{mol}$
List and compare the tissues that support and hold together theother tissues of the body.
options: Connective tissue is a single group of tissues that encapsulates organs without providing structural support.
The only function of connective tissue is to transport nutrients throughout the body.
Connective tissue is divided into two groups: bone and cartilage.
Connective tissue includes three types: muscle, nerve, and epithelial tissues.
Connective tissue is mainly composed of fluids and lacks solid structures like bone or cartilage.

Connective tissue functions to support and hold together structures of the body. They are classified into four groups by structure and/or function: bone, cartilage, blood, and fibrous connective tissue.
Connective tissue is categorized into five groups: bone, cartilage, blood, fibrous connective tissue, and adipose tissue.
Fibrous connective tissue doesn't contribute to the support and holding of other tissues.
Connective tissue only supports other tissues, it doesn't hold them together.
Connective tissues are not classified; all connective tissues have the same function and structure.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Connective tissue functions to support and hold together structures of the body. They are classified into four groups by structure and/or function: bone, cartilage, blood, and fibrous connective tissue.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following statements is correct concerning the "reasonable person" standard in tort law?
options: The reasonable person standard applies only to people with a high level of education.
The reasonable person standard applies only to people without disabilities.
The reasonable person standard varies from person to person.
The reasonable person standard requires that a person act as would the average person in the community.
A person with a mental disability must act as would a person with the same mental disability.
A person with a physical disability must act as would a reasonable person with the same disability.
The reasonable person standard focuses on the defendant's subjective mental state rather than on the defendant's behavior
The reasonable person standard is based solely on the defendant's age.
A person with a mental disability is not subject to the reasonable person standard.
attempted_job.correct_answer: A person with a physical disability must act as would a reasonable person with the same disability.

attempted_job.description: Use partial fraction expansion to find the inverse z-transform of F (z) = [{2z^2 - 2z} / {(z - 3) (z - 5)^2}].

Use the following key to translate the given formula of PL to natural, English sentences.
A: Marina reads a Percy Jackson book.
B: Izzy plays Minecraft.
C: Emily stops working.
D: Russell makes dinner.
E: Ashleigh stops by.
(~A ∨ ~B) ⊃ C
options: If Marina doesn't read a Percy Jackson book and Izzy doesn't play Minecraft, then Emily will stop working.
If Emily stops working, then either Marina doesn't read a Percy Jackson book, or Izzy doesn't play Minecraft.
Emily will stop working only if Marina doesn't read a Percy Jackson book and Izzy doesn't play Minecraft.
Emily stopping working is a sufficient condition for either Marina not reading a Percy Jackson book or Izzy not playing Minecraft.
Unless Marina reads a Percy Jackson book or Izzy plays Minecraft, Emily will stop working.
If Emily stops working, then neither Marina is reading a Percy Jackson book nor Izzy is playing Minecraft.
Either Marina not reading a Percy Jackson book or Izzy not playing Minecraft entails that Emily stops working.
If Marina reads a Percy Jackson book or Izzy plays Minecraft, then Emily won't stop working.
If neither Marina reads a Percy Jackson book nor Izzy plays Minecraft, then Emily stops working.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Either Marina not reading a Percy Jackson book or Izzy not playing Minecraft entails that Emily stops working.

options: Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00802lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 22.64 percent, Heat added: 5.1 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00602lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 23.64 percent, Heat added: 6.1 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00602lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00502lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 26.50 percent, Heat added: 6.5 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00752lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00702lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 27.50 percent, Heat added: 7.4 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00802lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00902lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 21.64 percent, Heat added: 8.1 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00702lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00852lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 20.64 percent, Heat added: 7.8 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00652lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 25.14 percent, Heat added: 6.0 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00702lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 24.64 percent, Heat added: 7.1 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00652lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 23.14 percent, Heat added: 6.6 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00502lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00602lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 28.64 percent, Heat added: 5.5 Btu/lbm of dry air
attempted_job.correct_answer: Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00602lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 23.64 percent, Heat added: 6.1 Btu/lbm of dry air

attempted_job.description: Calculate the density of the tightly coiled tropocollagen molecule, which may be considered to be a cylinder 2,800 \AA long and 14 \AA in diameter. It contains three poly-peptide chains of 1,000 amino acid residues each.
If an atom remains in an excited state for 10^-8 sec, what is the uncertainty in the energy of that state?

Statement 1| After mapped into feature space Q through a radial basis kernel function, 1-NN using unweighted Euclidean distance may be able to achieve better classification performance than in original space (though we can’t guarantee this). Statement 2| The VC dimension of a Perceptron is smaller than the VC dimension of a simple linear SVM.
What does EXE do when the STP solver times out on a constraint query for a particular path?
options: Assume that the query is not satisfiable and stop executing the path
Remove a subset of the constraints and retry the query.
Pause the execution until the query is resolved.
Skip the query and continue executing the path.
Assume that the query is satisfiable and continue executing the path.
Assume that the query is satisfiable and stop executing the path.
Restart STP and retry the query, up to a limited number of retries.
Execute the next path without resolving the query.
Retry the query until it is resolved, regardless of the number of retries.
Send an error message and terminate the program.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Assume that the query is not satisfiable and stop executing the path

attempted_job.description:
Let P be a procedure that for some inputs calls itself (i.e., is recursive). If P is guaranteed to terminate, which of the following statements must be true?
I. P has a local variable.
II. P has an execution path where it does not call itself.
III. P either refers to a global variable or has at least one parameter.
options: P has an execution path where it does not call itself and refers to a global variable
P has a local variable and refers to a global variable

Lancelot pauses onethird of the way up the ladder.  Find the magnitude of the contact force on the base of the ladder. (Unit: N)

Zoey is laying bricks for her patio. The salesman wants to sell Zoey as many bricks as possible to cover her patio with a thickness of one brick, while not having any extra bricks. The patio area is a rectangle with dimensions 12 feet by 10 feet, and each individual brick is 4 inches by 6 inches by 2 inches. What would be the greatest number of bricks the salesman could sell to meet his sales criteria?

A man buys a house and lot for $35,000, paying $12,000 down and borrowing the balance on a 6% mortgage due in 10 years. He pays real-estate taxes of $240, a water tax of $30, and insurance premiums of $70 a year. Allowing 4% interest on his investment, and 2% depreciation on the house valued at $25,000, what is the cost per month of owning the home if repairs average $120 a year?
If a test has a standard error of measurement of 15 points, itis correct to conclude that
options: about 85% of the observed scores for the test population lie within 15 points of the mean score
differences of up to 15 points represent the same true score
about 68% of the individuals with a given cure score will have observed scores within 15 points of the true score
about 68% of the observed scores for the test population lie within 15 points of the mean score
about 68% of the observed scores for the test population lie within 30 points of the mean score
about 68% of the observed scores will lie between 15 points below and above the true score
about 32% of the individuals with a given cure score will have observed scores within 15 points of the true score
about 15% of the individuals with a given score will have observed scores within 15 points of the true score
differences of up to 30 points represent the same true score
about 15% of the scores are in error (i.e., they are not measuring the trait reliably)
attempted_job.correct_answer: about 68% of the individuals with a given cure score will have observed scores within 15 points of the true score

attempted_job.description: A 20-month old clings nervously to her mother and does not want to explore a new play environment. She suddenly pushes her mother away and does not engage in any hugs her mother tries to give her. The infant’s facial expression shows anxiety but she looks away from her mother. The mother turns away from the baby as well. This child most likely has which type of attachment to her mother?
Calculate the percentage difference between $\ln (1+x)$ and $x$ for $x=0.0050$
